seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: OpenAI Assistants API ai email assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with OpenAI's Assistants API enables intelligent email processing, intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows in a managed agent runtime. However, its powerful code interpreter, file search, and function calling introduce risks like PII leaks, inappropriate tone, and hallucinated actions in high-stakes email scenarios. Vex provides essential verification guardrails to ensure safe, reliable operation.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses OpenAI Assistants API to create a persistent thread per email conversation. Incoming emails trigger the assistant via webhook or polling, leveraging built-in tools: code interpreter for attachment analysis, file search for email history/templates, and function calling for intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows. The assistant processes emails through a run cycle: receiving input, tool execution, and output generation with submit_message() for responses.

Key components include: an Assistant configured with email-specific instructions, tools for SMTP integration/email validation, and vector stores for recipient/knowledge base search. Runs complete via webhooks that trigger Vex verification before final actions like sending emails. This architecture handles threading, state management, and tool orchestration natively while exposing completion events for safety checks.

Risks emerge from unbounded thread context, unfiltered tool outputs, and hallucinated function arguments, making runtime verification critical for production email agents.

risks.name: PII Exposure in Email Drafts
risks.description: Code interpreter or function calls extract sensitive data (SSNs, emails) from attachments and include in drafts without filtering
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection scanner on all generated email content before sending

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Generation
risks.description: Assistant produces unprofessional, offensive, or overly casual language unsuitable for business recipients
risks.mitigation: Vex tone analysis ensuring professional communication standards

risks.name: Invalid Recipient Hallucination
risks.description: Function calling hallucinates email addresses outside validated domain lists
risks.mitigation: Vex recipient validation against corporate directory before SMTP submission

risks.name: Thread Context Overflow
risks.description: Growing conversation history exceeds token limits, causing silent truncation and context loss
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers summarization/archive workflows

implementation.title: Extract Verified Output
implementation.description: Retrieve assistant messages after run completion for Vex verification
implementation.code:
messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)
draft_content = None
for msg in messages.data:
    if msg.role == 'assistant':
        draft_content = msg.content[0].text.value
        break

# Send to Vex verification before SMTP
